headline: Mag 7 loses $2.3T but investors still back chipmakers
detail: This is the most direct headline for your book. AI spending anxiety is hammering the mega-cap software/hardware names, but the market is making a distinction - chipmakers are still seeing support. Your SMH, SOXL, and CRWD all live in this crossfire.
rec: No action yet - the 'still backing chipmakers' part is encouraging for SMH and SOXL, but with SOXL already down 7% on margin, wait for the AI jitter narrative to resolve before adding. Watch SMH around $540 stop as your cleaner, non-leveraged read on sector health.

headline: H2 AI trade could power higher - wouldn't take much
detail: This suggests the AI rally may be coiled rather than broken. If sentiment flips positive, your heaviest exposures - CRWD, SMH, SOXL, QQQ - would benefit the most and fastest. On margin, that cuts both ways.
rec: CRWD is the one name where this headline plus the scanner setup align: scanner shows entry 742.17-741.42, stop 740.31, Daily/4H/1H all up. You already own it at +3.26% - if price holds that entry zone, it's a reasonable add-to, but keep the stop tight given margin exposure.

headline: Oil prices set for steep monthly drop; Hormuz reopening faster than expected
detail: Falling oil and a reopening Hormuz strait eases inflation pressure and supply-chain anxiety. That's a mild macro tailwind for tech multiples - lower energy costs reduce data center operating expenses and keep rate-hike fears quieter.
rec: Positive background noise for your sector but not a direct trigger to buy or sell anything in your book today. Just a small tailwind, not a catalyst.

headline: Digital Realty falls 5% on $3.5B Blackstone data center deal
detail: A major data center REIT dropping 5% on a big AI infrastructure deal signals the market is nervous about capital expenditure levels in the AI buildout. That same capex anxiety is what's weighing on Mag 7 and, by extension, chip demand expectations.
rec: No direct holding affected, but this reinforces the AI spending jitter theme hurting your sector. It's a reason to stay cautious on adding to SOXL or SMH right now rather than a reason to trim what's already working.
